options.name: --branch
options.description:
Run the Blueprint version from a remote Git 
branch

options.name: --commit
options.description:
Specify a specific Commit ID. This is used in order to 
run a environment from a specific Blueprint historic version. If this parameter 
is used, the Branch parameter must also be specified.

options.name: --detail
options.description: Show detailed output in a json format

options.name: --duration
options.description:
The Environment will automatically de-provision at the 
end of the provided duration (minutes).

options.name: --input

metadata.name: ClrType
metadata.value: System.Collections.Generic.IDictionary`2[[System.String,  System.Private.CoreLib, Version=9.0.0.0, Culture=neutral,  PublicKeyToken=7cec85d7bea7798e],[System.String, System.Private.CoreLib,  Version=9.0.0.0, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=7cec85d7bea7798e]]

options.description:
The Blueprints inputs can be provided as a key=value 
pair. For example: -i key1=value1 -i key2=value2.

options.name: --name
options.description:
Provide a name for the Environment. If not set, the name 
will be generated automatically using the source branch (or local changes) and 
current time.

options.name: --repo
options.description: The repository name to use with command

options.name: --timeout
options.description:
Set how long (default timeout is 30 minutes) to block and
wait before releasingcontrol back to shell prompt. If timeout is reached before 
the desired statusthe wait loop will be interrupted.

options.name: --wait
options.description:
Block shell prompt and wait for the Environment to be 
Active while the timeout is not reached.Default timeout is 30 minutes.The 
default timeout can be changed using the 'timeout' flag.

arguments.name: blueprint-name
arguments.description:
The bluerpint name to run an envrironment 
from
